subject: Physics
title: The Higgs Boson at CERN
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: The Higgs Boson at CERN.

hook.text: On July 4, 2012, CERN announced the discovery of the Higgs boson—the last missing piece of the Standard Model.

buildup.text: The Large Hadron Collider smashed protons at 99.999999% light speed, creating trillions of collisions.

discovery.text: Among the debris, a particle at 125 GeV appeared with exactly the properties predicted by Peter Higgs in 1964.

twist.text: Finding it required two detectors with 100 million sensors each, and thousands of scientists from 100 countries.

climax.text: Higgs and Englert won the 2013 Nobel Prize. The particle that gives mass was finally confirmed.

punchline.text: 48 years from prediction to proof. The particle that gives everything mass.

quiz.question: How long was the gap between the Higgs boson prediction and its discovery?
